name: Declarative Single-Page Application
summary: A fully interactive SPA using only declarative HTML with zero JavaScript bootstrap or custom elements. Demonstrates maximum interactivity through pure markup and Web Everything standards.
description:
Concept

This demo explores the limits of declarative, zero-JavaScript interactivity by building a complete single-page application that is served as static HTML in the initial HTTP request. No framework bootstrap, no custom elements registration, no JavaScript imports—just HTML enhanced with Web Everything attributes.

Key Constraints

  • No JavaScript Bootstrap: The HTML document loads and is interactive immediately without waiting for JS bundles.
  • No Custom Elements: Uses only native HTML elements enhanced with behavioral attributes.
  • Server-Rendered: The complete application structure is returned in the initial HTTP response.
  • Progressive Enhancement: Core functionality works before any JavaScript loads; behaviors enhance the experience.

Interactive Features

  • State Management: Declarative context definitions using <script type="context">
  • Data Binding: Two-way binding with bind-text, bind-value attributes
  • Event Handling: Actions triggered via on:click, on:input without inline JavaScript
  • Conditional Rendering: Elements shown/hidden based on context state
  • List Rendering: Dynamic lists generated from context data
  • Form Validation: Declarative validation rules without custom code

Technical Approach

The demo leverages Web Contexts for state management, Web Directives for declarative behaviors, and Web Injectors for dependency resolution—all configured through HTML attributes and inline context definitions. Behaviors are attached via attribute-based protocols that activate on page load, transforming static HTML into an interactive application.

name: Router Demo
summary: Declarative client-side routing with guards, loaders, error boundaries, and named outlets — all driven by HTML templates inside a single <route-view> element.
description:
Concept

This demo showcases a fully declarative client-side router built on the Navigation API and URLPattern. Routes are defined as <template route="/path"> children of a <route-view> element, with matched content stamped in-place.

Key Features

  • Declarative Routes: Define routes as <template> elements with a route attribute — no JavaScript router configuration needed.
  • Route Guards: Protect routes with route:guard attributes. Guards can allow, block, or redirect navigation.
  • Route Loaders: Fetch async data before rendering via route:loader. Loader results populate the stamped content.
  • Loader Intent: Declare loading strategy (soft, hard, replacement, optimistic) and timing (debounced, immediate) via customContexts:loaderIntent. Switch strategies live to see different loading behaviors. Per-route overrides via route:loader-strategy attribute.
  • Error Boundaries: Define route:error templates to handle loader failures gracefully.
  • Named Outlets: Render auxiliary content in <route-outlet> elements for sidebars, toolbars, etc.
  • Navigation Links: route:link behavior on <a> elements intercepts clicks for SPA navigation with active class management.
  • Prefetching: route:prefetch="hover" warms the loader cache on hover for instant navigation.

Technical Approach

The router uses the Navigation API (with History API fallback) for URL interception, URLPattern for route matching, and the Web Everything injector system for guard/loader resolution. Templates are stamped and unstamped as the user navigates, with route context available to event handlers via custom events. Loading states are driven by the Loader Intent protocol — a single declaration that controls how pending states affect the UI.

name: View + Tabs Demo
summary: ViewEngine for show/hide with multiple hidden modes, exclusive groups, and toggle events. TabGroupBehavior for accessible tabbed interfaces with ARIA roles and keyboard navigation.
description:
Concept

This demo showcases the View and Tabs blocks — the foundation for all show/hide patterns in Web Everything.

Key Features

  • ViewEngine: Shared primitive for show/hide/toggle with 4 hidden modes (display, content-visibility, until-found, inert).
  • Toggle Events: Popover API-compatible beforetoggle (cancelable) and toggle events.
  • Exclusive Groups: Elements with the same name attribute — only one visible at a time, mirroring <details name>.
  • Tabs: Accessible tabbed interface with ARIA roles (tablist/tab/tabpanel), keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Home/End), and automatic/manual activation modes.
  • Configuration: Live switching between activation modes (automatic/manual) and orientations (horizontal/vertical).

Technical Approach

Tabs is a thin ARIA + keyboard layer over ViewEngine. Panels are always in the DOM (behavior pattern), with visibility managed by the engine. State preservation is automatic since elements are never destroyed.
